description: Letter from an unknown writer, probably in Hebron, to his wife (addressed as al-Sitt al-Jalīla), probably in Fustat. Addressed to al-Shaykh al-Muhadhdhab al-Khayyāṭ (the tailor), possibly the wife's father. In Judaeo-Arabic. The writer reports that he developed a serious illness (ḍuʿf ṣaʿb) in Hebron. He is sustained only by her prayers and his knowledge of her righteousness. He departed on this journey because business had ground to a halt and he needed to gain a living. (Information in part from Goitein’s note cards.) EMS. ASE.
tags: Illness, illness: du'f, illness: prayer, illness: ghurba, illness letter 969-1517, distant husband, Hebron
description_he: מכתב מאיש כנראה בחברון לאשתו (אל-סיתת אל-גלילה) בפוסטאט: חלה בחולי קשה בחברון ונסמך על תפילותיה. יצא לדרך כי העסקים דשכו. ביהודית-ערבית.
